tool_name: Steel Column Axial Compression — Slenderness, Critical Stress & Euler Buckling Load

inputs.name: Factored Axial Compressive Demand (P_u / N_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Effective Column Length (KL)
inputs.note: Effective length = K × L; AISC Commentary C-C2.2: pinned-pinned K=1.0; fixed-pinned K=0.7; fixed-fixed K=0.5

inputs.name: Minimum Radius of Gyration (r_min)
inputs.note: From section tables; r_y for wide-flange; r_min for angle — typically controls buckling

inputs.name: Steel Yield Strength (F_y)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: End Condition — Effective Length Factor (K)
inputs.note: Used if KL not pre-calculated; L_eff = K × physical length
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: KL_r = L_eff_m * 1000 / r_min_mm   // [dimensionless] Slenderness Ratio (KL/r) Reference: AISC 360-22 E2 — slenderness ratio; limit KL/r ≤ 200 for compression members
E_steel = 200000; Fe_mpa = Math.pow(Math.PI, 2) * E_steel / Math.pow(KL_r, 2)   // [MPa] Elastic Buckling Stress (F_e) Reference: AISC 360-22 E3-4 — F_e = π²E/(KL/r)²; E = 200,000 MPa for steel

lambda_c = Math.sqrt(Fy_mpa / Fe_mpa); Fcr_mpa = (lambda_c <= 1.5) ? Math.pow(0.658, lambda_c * lambda_c) * Fy_mpa : (0.877 / (lambda_c * lambda_c)) * Fy_mpa   // [MPa] Critical Compressive Stress (F_cr) — AISC LRFD Reference: AISC 360-22 E3 — λ_c ≤ 1.5: inelastic; λ_c > 1.5: elastic Euler range
phi_Pn_kn = 0.90 * Fcr_mpa * A_g_mm2 / 1000   // [kN] Nominal Column Capacity (φP_n) Reference: AISC 360-22 §E1 — φ_c = 0.90 (LRFD); P_n = F_cr × A_g
DCR = Pu_kn / phi_Pn_kn   // [dimensionless] Demand-Capacity Ratio (DCR) Reference: AISC 360-22 §H1-1 — DCR ≤ 1.0 required; DCR > 1.0 = structural inadequacy

I_min_mm4 = A_g_mm2 * Math.pow(r_min_mm, 2); Pcr_kn = Math.pow(Math.PI, 2) * 200000 * I_min_mm4 / Math.pow(L_eff_m * 1000, 2) / 1000   // [kN] Euler Critical Load (P_cr) — Elastic Buckling Reference: Euler: P_cr = π²EI/L_eff² — EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.1; reference for elastic buckling check

V1.error_msg: Column overstressed: axial demand exceeds φP_n — increase section area, reduce effective length, or reduce axial load

V2.error_msg: KL/r > 200: exceeds AISC 360 maximum slenderness limit for compression members — add intermediate bracing or select deeper section

V3.error_msg: Elastic buckling stress < 50% of yield: slender column dominated by buckling — verify effective length assumptions and add lateral bracing

smart_warnings.message: DCR > 90%: minimal reserve capacity — no additional loads or load combination factors can be accommodated; redesign before construction
Very slender column (KL/r > 150): highly sensitive to eccentricity and initial imperfections — apply additional amplification per AISC Appendix 8 or second-order analysis
High-strength steel with moderate slenderness: F_cr may be governed by elastic buckling, not yield — verify cost benefit of S460 vs S355 at this slenderness level
Applied load > 80% of Euler critical load: very close to elastic bifurcation point — P-delta effects and geometric imperfections become critical; perform second-order analysis
Cantilever column (K=2.0) over 5 m: very high effective length — verify fixed base connection rotational stiffness; partial fixity significantly increases effective K

fmea.effect: Column overstressed: axial demand exceeds φP_n — increase section area, reduce effective length, or reduce axial load
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: KL/r > 200: exceeds AISC 360 maximum slenderness limit for compression members — add intermediate bracing or select deeper section
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Elastic buckling stress < 50% of yield: slender column dominated by buckling — verify effective length assumptions and add lateral bracing
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: DCR > 90%: minimal reserve capacity — no additional loads or load combination factors can be accommodated; redesign before construction
Very slender column (KL/r > 150): highly sensitive to eccentricity and initial imperfections — apply additional amplification per AISC Appendix 8 or second-order analysis
High-strength steel with moderate slenderness: F_cr may be governed by elastic buckling, not yield — verify cost benefit of S460 vs S355 at this slenderness level
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Fy_mpa > 355 && KL_r > 80

fmea.failureMode: Applied load > 80% of Euler critical load: very close to elastic bifurcation point — P-delta effects and geometric imperfections become critical; perform second-order analysis
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Pu_kn > Pcr_kn * 0.8

fmea.failureMode: Cantilever column (K=2.0) over 5 m: very high effective length — verify fixed base connection rotational stiffness; partial fixity significantly increases effective K
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: end_condition === 2.0 && L_eff_m > 5
